subject: reduce ccsdk-ansible-server image footprint
commit_message:
reduce ccsdk-ansible-server image footprint

Project "CIA" scope is to provide smaller and multi architecture docker
images.

This proposition aims to do that for ccsdk-ansible-server.

The current size of this proposal is 139mb compared to the 618Mb image
proposed before (and before actual ansible installation).

Principles used are the following:

1. a "basic" `docker build .` should provide a working image;
2. use the smallest (and multi arch) base image possibe;
3. install necessary stuff only;
4. leverage python best practices for installation;
5. remove installation stuff occuring at run level and move them to
   build level.

* Dockerfile is now at the root part;
* ansible configuration file `ansible.cfg` is created and put in
  `configuration` folder;
* ansible configuration file `ansible.cfg` is pushed during the
  build part with a reproductible process;
* `python` files are on `ansible-server" folder so copying during build
  works out of the box;
* yml files are put directly on `ansible-server/Playbooks` so copying
during build works out the box;

* Dockerfile base image is now alpine:3.8;
* `timeout` executable being now from busybox, change in the
  command-line call has been made (`--signal` is now `-s` and `-t` is
  set before setting the timeout value).

* Only install python and python-pip as alpine package and remove build
  dependencies package after use.

* put all python requirements in `requirements.txt` package and launch
  pip installation command with this file.

* call directly python main script (`RestServer.py`) as entrypoint and
  do the python installation stuff at build time.

- [x] change pom.xml so building parts works
- [ ] consider changing destination folder (`/opt/ansible-server/`) to
      the ancient one (`/opt/onap/ccsdk/`) if this is problematic

Change-Id: I5c66f29ca40d9224aef3d230593735b8e0aa1f3b
Issue-ID: INT-816
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Not entirely happy with the directory structure changes - this was unnecessary - but willing to live with it for now to get the benefit of the other changes.  Might refactor back if this works okay, but am concerned that pip install at docker build time might be problematic (it was back when we first implemented this due to firewall issues, but that might have been before ONAP Jenkins moved to AWS - so could be fine now, and if so, that is definitely a far, far better method  then the workaround of installing at run time)

Patch Set 1:

Ok, got that: 
ERROR] Failed to execute goal io.fabric8:docker-maven-plugin:0.16.5:build (generate-images) on project ansible-server: Unable to build image [onap/ccsdk-ansible-server-image]: The command '/bin/sh -c apk add --no-cache py2-pip                        python2 &&    apk add --no-cache --virtual .build-deps build-base                                              libffi-dev                                              openssl-dev                                              python2-dev &&    pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip==$PIP_TAG &&     pip install --no-cache-dir -r ansible-server/requirements.txt &&    apk del .build-deps' returned a non-zero code: 1 -> [Help 1]


I add this error also when I was testing and the problem was this part of pom.xml:
<properties>
   ...
   <docker.buildArg.https_proxy>${https_proxy}</docker.buildArg.https_proxy>


When removed, it worked for me.
I can remove it in order to see if it works but do I need to recreate a review?

Patch Set 1:

Hi, Do we have any reason why moving to /opt/ansible-server? This moving would impact some scripts to provide the on-boarding VNF function for usecase.

Patch Set 1:

Well I moved to /opt/ansible-server as it's the name of the project. there's no particular reason.

I don't see why that would break something as it seems on my tests that path are relatives

Patch Set 1:

docker-maven-plugin version must be 0.28.0 to build on Arm (and absolute path of <dockerFile> must be removed)

Patch Set 1:

when we reshuffled the path and entry point. we have to check docker-compose file and Chart under OOM - that all breaks now.

Patch Set 1:

/bin/bash should be standard shell in container.

Patch Set 1:

hello @Takamune,
first, forcing entry point in an helm chart is a bad practice and here there were no need to do that.
Second, I asked Dan if he wanted that I update helm chart (https://jira.onap.org/browse/SDNC-639).
I also don't see the need for bash as we only need to start a python script.
I propose to update both ansible-server charts (for APPC and SDNC) in order to get rid of it and use the default entry point

Patch Set 1:

Why do you think a ENTRY point in helm Chart and docker compose is bad?

We need to change all helm Charts and docker compose files in ONAP in this gerrit that got impacted to make sure all working as well. 

I need bash for some scripts, which I will push to ansible container later. bash is standard shell script in linux for years. I do not know why alpine does not have bash. 

we can talk further. @Sylvain

Patch Set 1:

Guys, there is a problem with AnsibleModule.py. The file is different than the one was added with this commit https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/66131/, reverted with this gerrit https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/77516/. In consequence after commit of Sylvain ansibleSysCall from AnsibleModule.py is not compatible with ansibleSysCall  used in RestServer.py
